note: Curated queue of single, auto-settleable, always-placeable markets. The AI autopilot consumes these in kickoff order; the site shows them as the picks board. Keep selections to auto types (over_goals/under_goals/btts/team_win/double_chance) so they settle themselves.

picks.why: USA looked sharp going forward in their opener (4-1); enough to clear 1.5 in most games.
picks.whyRisk: Australia are organised and physical and can hold it to a 1-0 type game.

picks.why: Morocco (2022 semi-finalists) have the quality edge and shouldn't lose to Scotland.
picks.whyRisk: Scotland are well-drilled and can nick a result; a draw is fine for DC, a loss isn't.

picks.why: Brazil should overwhelm debutants Haiti; 3+ goals is the likeliest scoreline.
picks.whyRisk: Minnows can frustrate for ~60 mins, but quality usually tells.

picks.why: Germany's attack (7 vs Curaçao) should comfortably clear 1.5 goals.
picks.whyRisk: Ivory Coast defend well; a cagey low-scoring game is the only threat.

picks.why: Spain dominate possession and create high chance volume.
picks.whyRisk: Saudi may park the bus chasing a 1-0.

picks.why: Uruguay are far stronger than debutants Cape Verde and shouldn't lose.
picks.whyRisk: Low — only a genuine shock loss hurts.

picks.why: Both sides carry an attacking threat; Netherlands push the game.
picks.whyRisk: Sweden defend well and can keep it tight.
